Not only does this confirm that at least some dinosaurs had feathers — a theory that has already gained widespread acceptance — but it also provides details that fossils don't usually offer, such as colour and three-dimensional arrangement.
Scientists say the feathers of this animal were chestnut-brown on top and pale or carrot-colored on the bottom.
This discovery also helps deepen the understanding of the evolution of bird feathers.

The dinosaur's feathers lacked a well-developed central shaft—called a "rachis"—but still had other feather characteristics, such as barbs and barbules; researchers infer that the evolution of the rachis may have occurred later than these other traits.

The structure of the feathers indicates they were not used for flight, but for thermoregulation or display. Researchers believe that although this is the tail of a young dinosaur, the specimen shows mature plumage, not nestling down.

In France, voting is traditionally a low-tech experience: voters go into a booth, put a pre-printed slip of paper indicating their chosen candidate into an envelope.
After the officials verify the voter's identity, the voter drops the envelope into the ballot box and signs the voting register.

When a small group of organisms (a small population) becomes separated from their original main population (such as by crossing mountains and rivers, or arriving on a new island from which they cannot easily return), they often find themselves in an environment different from before.
This new environment has different resources and different competitors, so the new population will need different traits or adaptations to be a strong competitor, not the ones they needed before.
The original population hasn't changed at all, they still need to adapt to the environment as before.
Over time, the new population begins to adapt to the new environment, becoming less and less like the other populations.
After thousands or even millions of years, these two populations will eventually look so different that they can no longer be called the same species.
We call this process 'speciation,' which is the formation of new species. Speciation is an inevitable result of evolution and an extremely important part of it.

Duty-free shopping is the opportunity to buy tax-free goods at specific locations.

Travelers heading to countries or regions with heavy taxes can sometimes save a substantial amount of money, as purchasing products like alcohol and tobacco in these places is particularly cost-effective.
On the Buffalo-Pittsburgh Highway, the most challenging driving conditions are found on the stretch between Point Marion and Fairmont, which frequently passes through several remote outlying areas.
If you're not used to driving on country roads, keep your wits about you: there are steep grades, narrow lanes, and sharp turns everywhere.

The posted speed limit here is significantly lower than on the stretches before and after it. The common limit is 35–40 mph (56–64 km/h), and strict observance of the speed limit is more important here than on other sections.
However, strangely enough, cell phone service here is much stronger than along many other routes on the journey (such as the Pennsylvania Wilds).
German pastries are very good, and Bavarian desserts are even more diverse, closely resembling those of their southern neighbor, Austria.
Fruit desserts are very common, apples can be used to make desserts all year round, and in summer you can also enjoy cherries and plums.
Many German baked goods also feature almonds, hazelnuts, and other nuts. Popular cakes often pair perfectly with strong coffee.
If you want a small, richly flavored treat, try the jam-filled doughnut, a pastry known by different names across regions, such as Berliner, Pfannkuchen, Krapfen.
Curry is a dish primarily made with herbs and spices, served with meat or vegetables.
Curry dishes can be either "dry" or "wet", depending on the amount of water used.
In the inland regions of northern India and Pakistan, yogurt is often used in curries; whereas in southern India and other coastal areas of the subcontinent, coconut milk is often used.
Indonesia has 17,000 islands, so Indonesian cuisine is just an umbrella term that covers a wide variety of regional cuisines across the country.
But without further modifiers, the word tends to refer to food originating from the central and eastern parts of the main island of Java.

Now, Javanese food can be found all over the archipelago. Many of its dishes are simply seasoned, and the main seasonings Javanese people love are peanuts, chili peppers, sugar (especially Javanese coconut sugar), and various spices.
Stirrups hang on both sides of the saddle to support the rider's feet.
They provide better stability for the rider, but also pose a safety hazard, as the rider's foot may get caught.
If the rider is thrown from the horse but one foot becomes caught in the stirrup, the horse may drag him along while galloping. To minimize this risk, some precautions can be taken.
First, most riders wear heeled boots with smooth and fairly narrow soles.
Secondly, some saddles —particularly English saddles— are equipped with a safety catch, so the stirrup leather will come off the saddle if a fallen rider pulls it backward.
Chile's premier climbing destination, the Cochamó Valley (Cochamó Valley), is called the Yosemite (Yosemite) of South America, featuring a wide variety of massive granite big walls and cliffs.
From the peaks, there are breathtakingly magnificent views. Climbers from all over the world are constantly pioneering new routes on the endless steep cliffs that serve as climbing walls.
Alpine skiing includes regular skiing and snowboarding, and is a very popular sport, in which skis or a snowboard are attached to your feet and you slide down snow-covered terrain.
Skiing is a major tourist activity for many ski enthusiasts, who are sometimes referred to as "ski fanatics", planning their entire vacation to ski at one location.
The idea of skiing goes way back — cave paintings depicting skiers date back to 5000 BC!
As a sport, alpine skiing can be traced back to at least the 17th century, and in 1861, Norwegians opened the first recreational skiing club in Australia.
Ski touring: This activity is also called cross-country skiing, ski tourism, or ski hiking.

This activity is related to alpine ski touring or mountaineering, but typically does not involve these two activities. Those two activities take place on steep terrain and require stiffer skis and boots.
Think of the ski route as a similar hiking route.
In good conditions, you can go farther than on foot — but only in very rare situations can you achieve the same speed as cross-country skiing on a groomed track without a heavy backpack.
Europe is a relatively small continent with many independent countries. Usually, traveling across multiple countries means having to go through visa applications and passport checks multiple times.
In this respect, however, the Schengen Area is a bit like one country.
As long as you stay in this area, you can usually cross the border directly without having to go through passport control again.
Similarly, with a Schengen visa, you don't have to apply for a visa to each Schengen member state separately, saving time, money, and paperwork.
There is no uniform definition of which processed items are considered antiques. Some tax authorities define items over 100 years old as antiques.
This definition has regional differences, and the age limit in places like North America may be shorter than in Europe.
Handcrafted items may be defined as antiques, even though their age is much shorter compared to similar mass-produced goods.
Reindeer herding is an important livelihood for the Sami people. The culture surrounding this industry is also important to many who make a living from other occupations.

However, even in the traditional sense, not all Sami were involved in large-scale reindeer herding; they would make a living from fishing, hunting, and similar activities, and usually used reindeer as draft animals.
Today many Sami are engaged in modern industries. Tourism is a major source of income for Sápmi, that is, the Sami region.

Although the term 'Gypsy' is widely used, especially more prevalent among non-Roma, it is generally considered offensive because it is associated with negative stereotypes and misconceptions about Roma people.
If the country you are visiting is subject to a travel warning, your travel health insurance or travel cancellation insurance may be affected.
You can also consult the advice of governments other than your own, but their advice is intended for their own citizens.
For example, American citizens in the Middle East may face different circumstances than Europeans or Arabs.
Travel advisories can only provide a brief summary of the local situation.
Compared to the more detailed information provided elsewhere, the advice given here tends to be rather rough, general, and overly simplistic.
Severe weather is a general term for any dangerous weather phenomenon; such weather can cause destruction, serious social disruption, or casualties.
Severe weather can happen anywhere in the world. There are many different types of severe weather, depending on geographical location, terrain, and atmospheric conditions.
Gale-force winds, hail, excessive precipitation, wildfires, as well as thunderstorms, tornadoes, waterspouts, and cyclones are all manifestations and impacts of extreme weather.
Regional and seasonal severe weather phenomena include blizzards, snowstorms, ice storms, and sandstorms.
Travelers are strongly advised to be aware of severe weather risks in their location, as they may affect travel plans.
Anyone planning to travel to a country that may be considered a war zone should undergo professional training.
Search the internet for 'Hostile Environment Courses', maybe you will find addresses of local companies that provide training.
This course usually covers all the issues discussed here, often delving deeper with practical experience.
The training usually lasts 2-5 days and will involve role-playing, first aid training, and sometimes armed training.
Books and magazines about wilderness survival are quite common, but publications on war-zone survival are scarce.
Travelers planning to undergo gender reassignment surgery abroad must carry valid documents when returning.
As for the willingness to issue passports that do not specify a gender (X), or to amend documents to reflect the desired name and gender, governments' willingness varies from country to country.
The degree to which foreign governments recognize these documents also varies widely.
In the post-September 11, 2001 era, searches at security checkpoints also became more intrusive.
Transgender people who have not undergone surgery are forced to reveal their privacy when passing through scanners, and cannot safeguard their dignity.
A rip current is a backflow of waves that rapidly returns from the beach to the sea, usually occurring at reefs or similar places.
The underwater topography causes the backflow to concentrate in a few deeper areas, where a swift current heading into deep water may form.
Most deaths are caused by swimming against the current and exhaustion. Swimming against the current is unlikely to succeed.
After getting out of the swift current, swimming back is not that difficult.
Swim hard toward a place where you won't get trapped again, or wait for rescue (depending on your skills and whether you have been spotted).
The shock of returning home hits faster than culture shock (the honeymoon phase is shorter), lasts longer, and can be more severe.
For travelers who adapt easily to new cultures, it can sometimes be hard to readjust to their native culture.
After living abroad for a while and returning home, you'll find that you've adapted to the new culture and shed some habits of your home culture.
When you first go abroad, people might be very patient and understanding, because they know travelers need to adjust to a new country.
People may not realize that travelers also need patience and understanding on the return journey.
For children, the sound and light show at the pyramids is one of the most interesting activities in the area.
You can see the pyramids in the darkness, and before the show starts, you can see them standing there silently.
Normally, you can always hear the voices of tourists and street vendors here. The interweaving of light and sound is like telling a moving story.
On the backdrop is the Sphinx, which is also the teller of a long story.
The scene is displayed on the pyramids, with different pyramids lit up.

The South Shetland Islands were discovered in 1819. Several countries claim sovereignty over the archipelago, and it has the largest number of bases, with sixteen bases in operation on the islands in 2020.
The archipelago is located 120 kilometres north of the Antarctic Peninsula. The largest island is King George Island, home to the settlement of “Villa Las Estrellas”.
Others include Livingston Island and Deception Island. There, a still-active volcanic crater is flooded, providing a spectacular natural harbor.
Ellsworth Land is located in the southern part of the peninsula, facing the Bellingshausen Sea.
The mountain ranges of the peninsula merge here into the plateau, then re-form into the 360-kilometer-long Ellsworth Mountains, which are bisected by the Minnesota Glacier.
The northern part, or Sentinel Range, contains Antarctica's highest mountain range, the Vinson Massif, with its highest peak being Mount Vinson at 4,892 metres.
In remote areas without mobile phone coverage, a satellite phone may be your only choice.
Generally speaking, satellite phones cannot replace mobile phones, because calls can only be made outdoors where the satellite signal is unobstructed.
This service is often used in shipping, including yachts, and for expeditions requiring remote data and voice communication.
Your local telephone service provider should be able to provide detailed information about connecting to this service.
For those planning how to spend a gap year, study tours are becoming an increasingly popular choice.
It is especially popular among secondary school graduates, who can take a year out before university without interrupting their education.
In many cases, taking a gap year program abroad can substantially boost your chances of advancing to higher education back home.
Normally, participating in these educational programs requires paying tuition fees.
Finland is an excellent sailing paradise, known as the 'Land of a Thousand Lakes', with thousands of islands within its lakes and coastal archipelagos.
Among the archipelagos and lakes, a yacht is not a necessity.
The coastal archipelagos and the largest lakes are big enough to accommodate any yacht, yet smaller boats and even kayaks offer a different experience.
Boating is a national pastime in Finland, with one boat for every seven to eight people.
Norway, Sweden, and New Zealand have a similar situation, but are quite unique in other respects (for example, in the Netherlands, the figure is one to forty).
Most typical Baltic cruises make an extended stop in St. Petersburg, Russia.
This means you can have several full days to visit this historic city, then return to the ship to sleep at night.
If you only go ashore as part of a cruise, a separate visa is not required (regulation as of 2009).

Some cruise ships highlight Berlin, Germany, as a feature in their brochures. As can be seen from the map above, Berlin is not located near the sea, and the cruise fare does not include a tour of this city.
Air travel can be a terrifying experience for people of all ages and backgrounds, especially if they've never flown before or have had a traumatic experience.
It's nothing to be ashamed of: it's no different from the fear and disgust many people have towards other things.
For some people, learning about how airplanes work and the procedures of flight may help overcome fears that arise from unknown things or uncontrollable situations.
Courier companies can earn high rewards by delivering goods quickly. Often, time is very important for business documents, products, or parts needed for urgent repairs.
On some routes, large companies have their own planes, but for other routes and small companies, it becomes a problem.
If they use air freight to transport goods, on some routes, it might take a few days just to unload and clear customs.

The fastest way is to ship it as checked baggage. According to airline regulations, transporting the baggage of a passenger who hasn't boarded is prohibited, and that's when your presence is required.
The most obvious way to fly first or business class is to pay a hefty sum to enjoy that privilege (or, even better, have your company foot the bill).
However, it's not cheap: by rough estimates, a business class ticket can be 4 times the price of a regular economy class ticket, and a first class ticket can be as much as 11 times more!
Generally speaking, for a direct flight from A to B, there's absolutely no need to look for discounted business or first-class tickets.
Airlines are well aware that there are core passengers willing to pay a premium for the privilege of fast, comfortable travel, and they charge accordingly.
The capital of Moldova is Chișinău. The local language is Romanian, but Russian is also widely used.
Moldova is a multi-ethnic republic plagued by ethnic conflicts.

In 1994, this conflict led to the establishment of the self-proclaimed Transnistrian Republic in eastern Moldova, which has its own government and currency but is not recognized by any UN member state.
Despite failed political negotiations, economic ties between these two regions of Moldova were rebuilt.
The main religion of Moldova is Eastern Orthodoxy.
Izmir is the third largest city in Turkey, with a population of about 3.7 million. It is the second largest port in Turkey after Istanbul and a busy transportation hub.
Once the ancient city of Smyrna, it has now become a modern, developed and busy commercial center, surrounded by a huge bay and mountains.

Amid the wide, tree-lined boulevards, glass-fronted buildings and modern shopping malls, traditional red-tiled roofs, 18th-century markets and ancient mosques and churches are dotted around; yet the city has a more Mediterranean-European feel than traditional Turkey.
In the village of Haldarsvík, you can enjoy the beautiful view of the nearby island of Eysturoy. The village has an unusual octagonal church.
In the church cemetery, interesting marble dove sculptures are placed above some graves.
It is worth strolling for half an hour in this charming village.
To the north, one can easily reach the romantic and charming town of Sintra; it is a blessed land praised by Lord Byron, and thus well-known to foreigners.
The Scotturb 403 bus departs regularly for Sintra, stopping at Cabo da Roca.
You can also go north to visit the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fátima (holy site), the world-famous place where the Virgin Mary appeared.
Please remember, you are actually visiting the site of a huge mass grave, which holds immeasurable significance for a large group of people in the world.
Yet many men and women survived here, and are still alive today; while far more people's loved ones were either slaughtered there or worked to death as forced laborers, Jews and non-Jews alike.
Please give this place the dignity, solemnity, and respect it deserves. Do not joke about the Holocaust or Nazis.
Do not mark or graffiti on buildings, so as not to damage the heritage site.
The official languages of Barcelona are Catalan and Spanish. About half of the locals prefer to speak Catalan, the vast majority understand Catalan, and almost everyone understands Spanish.
However, most signs are only in Catalan, as it is the first official language by law.
However, Spanish is also widely used in public transport and other facilities.
Regular announcements on the metro are only in Catalan, but the automatic system makes temporary announcements in several languages, including Spanish, English, French, Arabic, and Japanese.
Parisians are notorious for being self-centered, rude, and arrogant.

Although this is usually just an inaccurate stereotype, the best way to live in Paris is still to behave as properly as possible, acting like a person with "bien élevé" (good breeding). This makes attending social events much easier.
If you show a little basic courtesy, the rudeness of Parisians will quickly disappear.
Plitvice Lakes National Park is densely forested, with beech, spruce, and fir as the main tree species, and is characterized by a mixture of alpine and Mediterranean vegetation.
Due to the diversity of microclimates, soils, and altitudes, the plant communities here are extremely rich in species, and are world-famous for it.
The region is also home to a variety of animals and birds.
Rare animals such as the European brown bear, wolf, eagle, owl, lynx, wildcat and capercaillie, as well as many more common species, can be seen there.
Women must wear skirts when visiting monasteries, the skirt must be knee-length, and shoulders must also be covered.
Most monasteries provide wrap dresses for women who are unprepared, but if you bring your own, especially brightly colored ones, the monk at the gate will give you a tight-lipped smile.
Similarly, men must wear long trousers that cover the knees.
You can also borrow them from the cloakroom at the entrance, but the clothes aren't washed after every use, so these skirts might be a bit uncomfortable to wear. They're all one size!
The cuisine of Mallorca, like that of similar Mediterranean regions, is primarily based on bread, vegetables and meat (especially pork), and uses olive oil exclusively.
Pa amb Oli is a simple supper especially popular in summer: bread with olive oil, tomato, and whatever you have on hand, such as cheese, tuna, and so on.
All nouns, except for "Sie" meaning "you" (formal), must begin with a capital letter, even in the middle of a sentence.
This is an important method for distinguishing certain verbs from objects.
This can also make reading easier, although it complicates writing to some extent, because you need to determine whether a verb or adjective is being used as a nominalized form.
Italian pronunciation is relatively easy, because most words are pronounced exactly as they are written.
The main letters to pay attention to are c and g, because their pronunciation changes based on the following vowels.
Also, make sure to pronounce r and rr differently: “caro” means “dear,” while “carro” means “chariot.”
Persian grammar is mostly relatively simple and follows regular patterns.
Therefore, reading this introductory grammar book will help you master more Persian grammar and better understand phrases.

However, people who know a little Spanish might jump to the conclusion that Portuguese is so close to Spanish that it doesn't need to be learned separately.

During their heyday, light pollution was not as severe as it is now, so observatories were usually built in urban areas or on campuses, making them more accessible than modern observatories.
Most modern research telescopes are giant facilities located in remote areas with excellent atmospheric conditions.
Cherry blossom viewing, also known as "hanami", has been a Japanese custom since the 8th century.
This concept originated from China, and the plum blossom is a flower beloved by the Chinese people.
In Japan, the earliest cherry blossom parties were held by the emperor for himself and other members of the nobility at the imperial residence.

If visiting a manicured garden, collecting “specimens” will also get you expelled, no exceptions.

Christmas is one of the most important holidays in Christianity, established to celebrate the birth of Christ.
topic: Reason to travel/Christmas and New Year

text: Many traditions associated with this holiday are also embraced by non-believers in Christian countries and by non-Christians around the world.
topic: Reason to travel/Christmas and New Year

text: There's a tradition of staying awake somewhere outdoors on Easter night to watch the sunrise.
Of course, this tradition has its Christian theological interpretation, but it was most likely a ritual of celebrating spring and praying for a good harvest that existed before the birth of Christ.

More traditional churches often hold an Easter Vigil on the Saturday evening of Easter weekend, with the congregation usually pouring into the celebration as the midnight bells toll, celebrating the resurrection of Christ.
Initially, animals reached these islands by swimming, flying, or floating.
Because they are far from the mainland, mammals could not travel long distances to reach them, making giant tortoises the main herbivores of the Galapagos Islands.
After humans arrived at the Galapagos Islands, many mammals were introduced, including goats, horses, cattle, rats, cats, and dogs.
If you travel to the Arctic or Antarctic region in winter, you can experience the polar night, a period when the sun does not rise above the horizon.
This provides an excellent opportunity to see the Northern Lights, as the sky will be dark more or less continuously throughout the day.
These areas are sparsely populated and generally free from light pollution, so you can also enjoy the brilliant starry sky.
Compared to the work culture that Westerners are accustomed to, the Japanese work culture has a more pronounced hierarchy and is more formal.
Suits are standard business attire, and colleagues address each other by their last names or job titles.
A harmonious workplace atmosphere is crucial, emphasizing team efforts rather than praising individual achievements.
Employees must obtain approval from their superiors for any decisions and must unconditionally obey the instructions of their superiors.
